confidence_rationale: Canonical review directly demonstrating that distinct representations nest within gamma subcycles of a theta cycle in hippocampal-prefrontal circuits — the precise packaging mechanism MECH-089 requires. High source quality; mapping to REE's ThetaBuffer is straightforward; main transfer risk is species/scale.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Gamma-frequency oscillations nest within theta cycles in the hippocampus; each gamma subcycle (~5-7 per theta cycle) carries a distinct spatial or sequential representation, implementing an ordered multi-item neural code.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-089: E3 never receives raw E1 prediction error — it receives a theta-cycle-averaged summary (ThetaBuffer). The gamma-within-theta nesting architecture is the biological substrate of the ThetaBuffer. Each E1 sensory frame (gamma-rate) is packaged into a rolling theta-cycle summary sampled at E3's deliberation rate.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Rodent hippocampal spatial coding; whether the same mechanism scales to cortical-like deliberative planning loops (E3-rate) and non-spatial representations requires additional corroboration. The theta-rate (~8 Hz) is faster than some views of E3 deliberation rate; rate mismatch should be acknowledged.
mapping.source_context: Review of hippocampal CA1/CA3 and hippocampal-prefrontal circuits in rodents; spatial working memory and sequential learning tasks.

confidence_components.notes: Strong, canonical source with broad empirical support; mapping to ThetaBuffer is structurally faithful; transfer risk is modest (scale and task-type differences).
